title: Financial Literacy Workshops
summary: Group sessions for organizations, teams, and community groups wanting a shared financial literacy workshop: budgeting, saving, or a custom topic.
audience: Employers, organizations, community and alumni groups, and schools.
overview: Financial Literacy Workshops bring the same plain-language, no-hype approach to a group setting, whether that's an employer wanting to support staff, a community or alumni group, or a school. Workshops are built around the group's context, typically covering budgeting fundamentals, saving habits, and an introduction to the investment options available to Ghanaians.
whoItsFor: You're an employer wanting to offer real financial-literacy value to your team
You're organizing a community, church, or alumni group session
You represent a school or student group wanting a practical session
You want a shared, group learning experience rather than individual sessions
whatYoullLearn: Budgeting fundamentals appropriate to the group's context
Practical saving habits, including mobile-money-based approaches
A plain-language introduction to Ghanaian investment options
Whatever custom topic is agreed for your specific group

process.title: Discuss your group
process.description: Size, context, and what the group most needs.

process.title: Agree on a topic and format
process.description: A workshop built around your group, not a fixed, generic deck.

process.title: Delivery
process.description: In person or virtual, depending on what works for your group.

process.title: Optional follow-up resources
process.description: Materials attendees can keep and revisit afterward.

faq.question: What size groups do you work with?
faq.answer: This varies. Share your group's size when you reach out, and we'll confirm whether the format fits.

faq.question: Can workshops be customized to our group's needs?
faq.answer: Yes, the topic and format are agreed together rather than delivered as a fixed, one-size-fits-all session.

faq.question: Are workshops in-person or virtual?
faq.answer: Either, depending on what works best for your group.

faq.question: Is there a minimum group size?
faq.answer: This is discussed directly when you reach out, based on your group's context.

faq.question: Can attendees get individual follow-up afterward?
faq.answer: Yes, attendees are welcome to explore Personal Financial Coaching or book their own consultation afterward.

faq.question: How do we book a workshop for our organization?
faq.answer: Book a consultation through the Contact page, and share your group's size and context so we can confirm a fit.

faq.question: Do workshops include investment recommendations?
faq.answer: No, workshops are educational, covering how things work rather than recommending specific products or investments.

complianceNote: Robayer WealthLab provides financial education, not licensed financial advice.
